verdicts.note: Quote 'Modalities: eeg, beh' is verbatim and present in e2, but it merely lists two modalities without designating either as 'primary'. The value 'eeg' is a plausible inference (eeg listed first, README is EEG-heavy) but the cited quote does not itself establish primacy.
Value is null with an appropriate 'needs' note and no evidence claimed. Nothing in e2 addresses clinical/health status, so leaving it null and unevidenced is correct.
Quote 'DatasetDOI: doi:10.18112/openneuro.ds008037.v1.0.0' appears verbatim in e2 and directly establishes the value.
Quote 'License: CC0' appears verbatim in e2 and directly establishes the value.
Quote 'Number of subjects: 123' appears verbatim in e2 and directly establishes the value.
Quote 'OpenNeuro dataset ds008037 (snapshot 1.0.0)' appears verbatim in e2 and establishes OpenNeuro as the provider.
Value is null with an appropriate 'needs' note and no evidence claimed; e2 contains no site/institution location.
Value is null with an appropriate 'needs' note and no evidence claimed; e2 never states 'human' explicitly, so leaving this unconfirmed is correct rather than asserting it.

Quote 'License: CC0' is verbatim but pertains to copyright/reuse licensing, not access/download gating. CC0 does not by itself establish that the dataset access mode is 'open' (e.g., registration or other access controls are not addressed one way or the other by this quote); it conflates license status with access status.
Value is null with an appropriate 'needs' note and no evidence claimed; e2 states no access restrictions or terms beyond the license line.
Value is null with an appropriate 'needs' note and no evidence claimed; e2 contains no pricing information.
The cited README sentence is verbatim in e2, and each list item is a faithful, non-distorting decomposition of substrings that appear verbatim within that sentence (verified programmatically).
All three cited quotes (License: CC0; Number of subjects: 123; the README description sentence) are verbatim in e2 and collectively support the summary's claims.
Spanish translation of the same facts covered by the English summary; underlying cited quotes are verbatim in e2 and support the translated content (no new claims introduced).
Value is null with an appropriate 'needs' note correctly distinguishing the upload/snapshot timestamp from an unstated data-collection period; no evidence claimed for a value.
